bug_report.summary: Firefox View: main content layout/styling changes unexpectedly (section header/content indentation and empty-state card presentation)
steps_to_reproduce: Enable `browser.tabs.firefox-view-next` = true in about:config.
Open Firefox View (next) via the Firefox View button.
Observe the 'Recent browsing' page with sections like 'Open tabs' and 'Recently closed tabs'.
Compare layout to previous build (before-change).
expected_behaviors: Aside from scrollbar/overflow fixes on extremely wide windows, the Firefox View main content layout should remain visually consistent (section headers and content alignment/padding unchanged; empty-state presentation unchanged).
actual_behaviors: Section header/content alignment changes: headings such as 'Recent browsing', 'Open tabs', and 'Recently closed tabs' appear shifted/indented differently (Parsed_Info: 0, 7, 10).
The 'Recently closed tabs' empty state appears rendered inside a different/nested card/container with altered padding/width (large region change across the empty-state area; Parsed_Info: 1, 13, 19).
Overall the main content area’s spacing/structure changes even though the change intent only mentions scrollbars/overflow behavior.

bug_report.summary: Firefox View: unexpected layout/styling changes when window is extremely wide (title/header alignment, accordion caret/indentation, and empty-state container padding)
steps_to_reproduce: Enable `browser.tabs.firefox-view-next` = true.
Open Firefox View (next) via the Firefox View button.
Resize the Firefox window to an extremely wide size (≥ 3000 px).
Observe the main content area: page title and section headers ('Open tabs', 'Recently closed tabs') and the 'Recently closed tabs' empty state.
expected_behaviors: Only overflow/scrollbar behavior should change per the fix (no unnecessary horizontal scrollbar; no redundant vertical scrollbars).
The Firefox View main content layout/styling (alignment of title/headers, indentation, and empty-state container/padding) should remain the same as before.
actual_behaviors: The 'Open tabs' area presents differently (accordion caret/indentation changes and different header/content alignment; Parsed_Info: 3,7,15).
The 'Recently closed tabs' empty-state block appears inside a different/nested container with altered padding/width (large content region change; Parsed_Info: 1,13,18).

bug_report.summary: Firefox View: Open tabs list columns appear rearranged (timestamps not aligned with their rows) at zoomed-out levels
steps_to_reproduce: Set browser.tabs.firefox-view-next=true.
Open about:firefoxview-next.
Zoom out (Ctrl+-) until UI is heavily zoomed out (e.g., ~67% or lower).
Observe the Open tabs card row layout (URL/title vs timestamp positioning).
expected_behaviors: Each Open tabs row should have consistent column layout, with timestamps (e.g., “Just now”) aligned in the appropriate column for the same row across all zoom levels.
actual_behaviors: The Open tabs card layout changes such that the URL/title and “Just now” timestamp placement appears rearranged/misaligned compared to before, making it harder to associate timestamps with the correct rows.

bug_report.summary: Firefox View: “View all” control placement/visibility changes unexpectedly in the Open tabs card at zoomed-out levels
steps_to_reproduce: Set browser.tabs.firefox-view-next=true.
Open about:firefoxview-next.
Zoom out (Ctrl+-) to ~67% or lower.
Look at the right side of the Open tabs card header/content area.
expected_behaviors: Scrollbar/overflow fixes should not introduce or reposition unrelated controls; if “View all” exists, its placement should be stable and not interfere with list/timestamp layout.
actual_behaviors: A “View all” link/control appears and/or is positioned differently than before within the Open tabs card area, coinciding with other layout shifts in the Open tabs region.

bug_report.summary: Firefox View (about:firefoxview-next): Open tabs row timestamp/metadata text changes or becomes unreadable at 30% zoom
steps_to_reproduce: Set browser.tabs.firefox-view-next=true.
Open about:firefoxview-next.
Zoom out to 30% (Ctrl+- until the zoom indicator shows 30%).
Observe the Open tabs rows’ timestamp/metadata on the right (optionally scroll within the main content).
expected_behaviors: Open tabs row metadata/timestamps (e.g., “Just now”, “1w ago”, etc.) should remain consistently visible/readable at extreme zoom-out and should not disappear or render inconsistently.
actual_behaviors: In the after-change UI at 30% zoom, the timestamp/metadata area renders differently than before (text content/visibility changes, appearing missing/blank or otherwise inconsistent compared to the before-build).

bug_report.summary: New Tab: Top Sites row shows blank/empty tiles (missing icon + label) after change
steps_to_reproduce: Open Firefox.
Open a New Tab (about:newtab).
Observe the Top Sites row on the right side.
expected_behaviors: Top Sites entries should render with a favicon (or fallback) and a site label for each visible tile.
If there are fewer Top Sites, the UI should not display empty placeholder tiles without labels.
actual_behaviors: One or more Top Sites tiles appear as empty/blank placeholders with no icon and no label (rightmost tiles).

false_positive_reasons: LLM hallucination — misinterpreted or imagined GUI rendering

bug_report.summary: Address bar autocomplete panel shows large empty area instead of shrinking to content
steps_to_reproduce: Open Firefox.
Open a New Tab (about:newtab).
Click the address bar to open the autocomplete/dropdown panel.
expected_behaviors: The autocomplete panel should size to its displayed results and footer controls (no large unused blank region).
actual_behaviors: A large blank white area is rendered below the “This time, search with:” row within the autocomplete panel, extending the panel height unnecessarily.

false_positive_reasons: LLM hallucination — misinterpreted or imagined GUI rendering

bug_report.summary: Welcome/Onboarding page content becomes blank after resizing window height very short
steps_to_reproduce: Open Firefox to the Welcome/Onboarding tab (mozilla.org/privacy/firefox… page shown).
Resize the Firefox window height to be very short (≤ 360 px, e.g., ~1200×340).
Observe the page hero illustration and the main text/buttons on the right.
expected_behaviors: Page content (illustration, headline/body text, and buttons like “Save and continue”, “Sign in”, “Skip this step”) should remain visible and readable after resizing, with responsive reflow as needed.
actual_behaviors: Main page content disappears: the hero illustration area becomes blank and the headline/body text and button labels are not visible (large empty placeholders/white areas instead).

bug_report.summary: Firefox View introduces nested vertical scrolling: 'Recently closed tabs' and 'Tabs from other devices' panels show internal scrollbars
steps_to_reproduce: Enable Firefox View (next) (browser.tabs.firefox-view-next=true).
Open Firefox View (next).
Observe the 'Recently closed tabs' and 'Tabs from other devices' sections at default window size.
expected_behaviors: Sections should not introduce additional internal vertical scrollbars unless necessary; overall scrolling should be handled by the main content area (avoiding nested/redundant scrollbars).
actual_behaviors: The 'Recently closed tabs' and 'Tabs from other devices' panels display their own vertical scrollbars inside the cards, creating nested scrolling behavior that was not present before.

false_positive_reasons: LLM hallucination — misinterpreted or imagined GUI rendering

bug_report.summary: Firefox View: unexpected disclosure/chevron markers appear next to section headers (Open tabs / Recently closed tabs / Tabs from other devices)
steps_to_reproduce: Enable Firefox View (next) via about:config pref browser.tabs.firefox-view-next = true
Navigate to about:firefoxview-next at a typical window size (e.g., ~1200×800)
expected_behaviors: Section headers should render without unintended default disclosure/marker icons (only intended icons/spacing).
actual_behaviors: New caret/chevron-like markers appear to the left of multiple section headers, changing the visual design and alignment (seen near the left edge of the Open tabs, Recently closed tabs, and Tabs from other devices headers).

false_positive_reasons: LLM hallucination — misinterpreted or imagined GUI rendering
